channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: CDC recommends lower age for people to get vaccine against pneumonia
item.description: All adults aged 50 and older should receive a vaccine against bacteria that can cause pneumonia and meningitis, federal health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Lifelong 'rivals' recall love and admiration for pitching legend Fernando Valenzuela
item.description: "Nobody will ever do what he did, we lost a living legend, he was me, he was you, he was our brothers, sisters, cousins, he was us," said Hernandez. "He represented us, he looked like us, talked like us, acted like us, and the loss is for every single one of us.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Students, families report overcrowded school buses in Utah
item.description: Families are raising concerns after students have reported overcrowded school buses in the Alpine School District.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Tracy, NewsNation

item.title: The EPA imposes stricter standards to protect children from exposure to lead paint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two weeks after setting a nationwide deadline for removal of lead pipes, the Biden administration is imposing strict new limits on dust from lead-based paint in older homes and child-care facilities.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ron Ely, TV's 'Tarzan' in the 1960s, dies at 86
item.description: Ron Ely, the tall, musclebound actor who played the title character in the 1960s NBC series “Tarzan,” has died at age 86.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas to begin gathering noncitizen treatment data from hospitals statewide
item.description: Governor Greg Abbott's Executive Order 46 goes into effect on November 1 and requires all public hospitals in Texas to ask their patients if they are U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video: Suspected drunken driver passes within feet of Harris' motorcade in Wisconsin
item.description: The driver was traveling in the wrong direction on the interstate in Milwaukee when deputies spotted him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Haddonfield, Illinois: How ‘Halloween’s’ iconic small town of horror came to be
item.description: Haddonfield, Illinois, a fictional small town, is the setting for John Carpenter's iconic horror film "Halloween," which has become a cultural phenomenon and has inspired numerous sequels and remakes.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: After 36 years, accused killer in Oregon cold case to stand trial
item.description: An autopsy revealed Deborah Atrops was physically assaulted and strangled to death. She was 30. Then, the case went cold.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect in shootings of Democratic office in Arizona had over 120 guns in his home, officials find
item.description: An Arizona prosecutor says the man arrested in the three-time shooting of a Democratic campaign office had more than 120 guns and over 250,000 rounds of ammunition in his home.
item.dc:creator: Anita Snow And Walter Berry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 2 arrested after man claims kidnappers amputated his finger in Denver
item.description: Denver police arrested two people in connection to an alleged kidnapping and assault wherein the victim's finger was amputated by his captors.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: Fallout from 2023 Hamas attack lingers as Jews worldwide prepare for a holiday meant for joy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Customarily, Simchat Torah is one of the most joyous days of the Jewish calendar, highlighted by exuberant dancing around a Torah. Jewish leaders say the joy will return during this week's celebrations, even as Jews recall that last year's Simchat Torah overlapped with Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana's 2020 Teacher of the Year is trying to block the state's Ten Commandments law
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's 2020 Teacher of the Year has filed a lawsuit against the state challenging a new law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1. It's the second lawsuit filed against the law, which was enacted in June.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 7,100 bottles of prescription antidepressants recalled over potentially cancer-causing substance
item.description: A pharmaceutical company issued a recall this month for thousands of bottles of duloxetine, a medicine often prescribed to treat depression.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WGN 9

item.title: Meet the Bitcoin believer who ditched stocks completely
item.description: If you thought Bitcoin already had its moment - think again. Recently, I attended LA Bitcoin Day, held at the upscale Casa Del Mar hotel in Santa Monica. The day-long event celebrates the "lifestyle, people, and businesses" building a "bright, orange future.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri woman finds trunk full of stolen campaign signs with help of Apple AirTag
item.description: "I expected to find the AirTag, but not 59 signs. It was kind of like finding a dead body," the Missouri woman said.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, Fox 5

item.title: Want to be a Power Ranger? Original costumes, props up for auction
item.description: It's been over 30 years since the original Mighty Morphin Power Rangers television series aired in the United States, and for the kids who grew up watching the Power Rangers fight evil in Angel Grove, California, their chance to become one of them is now closer than ever.
item.dc:creator: Marco Rosas, Fox 5

item.title: The Capitol Christmas Tree is coming to Utah as part of its 4,000-mile trek -- Here's how it's staying alive
item.description: Here's what you should know about this year's Capitol Christmas Tree — and how the tree will be kept alive during its travels.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: MLB wants the Tampa Bay Rays to play in the area if hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field is unfit
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Major League Baseball hopes to find the Tampa Bay Rays a temporary home near their fanbase at least for the start of the 2025 season if the damage to Tropicana Field from Hurricane Milton cannot be repaired in time. The translucent fiberglass dome at the ballpark in St.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These are the 'rattiest' cities in the US, according to Orkin's 2024 report
item.description: Chicago — which was briefly attached to a rat-shaped hole in a sidewalk this year — took the top spot again, marking a decade of ratty dominance.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles Times won't endorse Harris for president — or Trump
item.description: When Southern California residents go to fill out their ballots in the Nov. 5 election, they might rely on the Los Angeles Times' endorsements for guidance. But if you're looking for a recommendation for president from the Times, you're out of luck. Dr.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: US charges Iran Revolutionary Guard official in alleged plot to kill a human rights activist in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Tuesday charged a senior Iranian military official and three others with links to that country's government with plotting to kill an Iranian American author on U.S. soil — a development lauded by the alleged target. “This is a beautiful day for me.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in Walmart oven
item.description: A Walmart store in eastern Canada was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven. Halifax Nova Scotia Regional Police said in a release that they were called at 9:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Jocelina Joiner, KTLA 5

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New campus protest rules spur an outcry from college faculty
item.description: Dissent is thriving this fall at American colleges, and not just among student activists. With student protests limited by new restrictions, faculty have taken up the cause. To faculty, new protest rules threaten freedom of speech — and the freedom to think, both central to university life.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disgraced Hollywood producer Harvey Weinstein diagnosed with bone cancer: Reports
item.description: Disgraced movie producer Harvey Weinstein has been diagnosed with bone cancer, according to reports. The infamous Hollywood mogul’s diagnosis of chronic myeloid leukemia was first reported by NBC News on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Right whale population grows 4% but extinction remains a threat
item.description: BOOTHBAY HARBOR, Maine (AP) — One of the rarest species of whale in the world has increased slightly in population, encouraging conservationists to call on the federal government and the shipping and fishing industries to do more to bring the giant animals back from the brink of extinction.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Eyewitness video captures frantic efforts to save lives after deadly collapse of dock walkway
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The metal gangway where dozens of people waited to board a ferry boat made a loud, creaking noise before snapping in the middle amid panicked cries from those sent plunging into the water.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: White House public tour gets makeover with 'reimagined' visitor experience
item.description: The White House public tour is getting an extreme makeover, with new digital exhibits, an expanded route and more access to rooms poised to be part of the "reimagined" experience for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees and Dodgers meet in World Series for 12th time in matchup of Broadway and Hollywood
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Broadway vs. Hollywood. Subway vs. Freeway. Judge vs. Ohtani. New York neighbors who became cross-country rivals, the Yankees and Dodgers renew their starry struggle in the World Series for the first time in 43 years.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 6 Florida counties saw 'unusual increase' in flesh-eating bacteria after hurricanes: health officials
item.description: Florida health officials noted that six Tampa Bay area counties saw an "unusual increase" in cases of Vibrio vulnificus this year, attributed to floodwaters from back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WGN 9
